findings: W1 (warning, follow-up required): DUAL-mode JSONL twin is written before the superset guard fires, so a refused subset run still shrinks the JSONL tier of the pack; Parquet truth is protected and the scaleout incident vector is closed (PARQUET-only by config validation); fix is hoisting the guard to the top of both writers.

W2 (warning, follow-up): label pack writers (labels/fast/materializer.py:1450, labels/engine.py:804) remain unguarded for the same structural hazard class; out of this feature-scoped spec, needs its own phase before any label-side force-recompute data-op.

W3 (minor): reconcile_after_register kwarg on PackMaterializer.register_pack is an internal way to skip reconciliation; its single False caller reconciles at unit level afterward and it is not CLI-exposed, but future callers could silently skip.
W4 (minor): no explicit deprecate-then-rewrite test; the deprecated-first path is correct by construction (SQL lifecycle filter on REGISTERED rows) but untested.

mutation_tests.change: superset comparison disabled (if missing and False)

live_audit.source: ~/logs/alpha_pipeline/pack_audit_live.json (alpha scaleout pack-audit --json against live registry, ALPHA_DATA_ROOT=/home/yuke_zhang/alpha_data/alpha_system; CLI rc=2 on detected damage)
live_audit.caveat: Audit RACED the live repair operations (deprecate-first of 168 fvers at 10:32Z + full re-materialization 10:33-10:55Z); counts are a mid-repair snapshot that directionally confirms detection of all three damage classes on real damage, not the final state. Post-repair cross-check via tools/futures_substrate_scaleout/pack_restore_audit.py at 10:55Z: bbo 264/264 hash-identical, session 120/120, regime 72/72, stale=0.
